QofE Coordination Request
Quality of Earnings — CPA Support Required
CPA
{{cpaName}}
Firm
{{cpaFirm}}
Company
{{companyName}}
Date
{{sentDate}}
§ 01What We Need

Dear {{cpaName}},

Forhemit is facilitating an employee ownership transition (ESOP) for {{companyName}}. As part of the transaction, the independent ESOP trustee requires a Quality of Earnings (QofE) analysis to determine the fair market value of the company.

We are writing to request your cooperation in providing the financial data and working papers that the independent appraiser will need to complete the QofE analysis. Your firm prepared the financial statements and tax returns for the company — your involvement is critical to the accuracy and completeness of the appraisal.

§ 02What the QofE Appraiser Needs

The independent appraiser ({{appraiserName}}, {{appraiserFirm}}) has requested the following from the company's CPA:

A.3 years of tax returns. Federal corporate returns (1120 or 1065) for {{taxReturnYears}}. All schedules, K-1s, and attachments.
B.3 years of financial statements. P&L and balance sheet for {{financialYears}}. CPA-prepared, reviewed, or compiled — indicate level of assurance.
C.Year-to-date financials. Current year P&L and balance sheet through {{ytdDate}}.
D.Working papers. Any adjusting journal entries, reclassifications, or normalizations made during the preparation of the financial statements.
E.Chart of accounts. Current chart of accounts with account descriptions.
F.Revenue breakdown. Revenue by customer, product line, or service line for the most recent 3 years. The appraiser needs to assess revenue concentration and recurrence.
G.Expense detail. Detailed expense breakdown for the most recent 3 years, including any owner-related or non-recurring items that should be normalized.
H.Owner compensation. Total compensation (salary, benefits, distributions, personal expenses run through the business) for all owners/officers for the most recent 3 years.
§ 03Normalization Adjustments

The QofE appraiser will normalize the financials to reflect the company's true earning power. Please flag any items you are aware of that may require normalization:

1.Above-market owner compensation. If owner compensation exceeds market rate for the role, the excess is added back to EBITDA.
2.Personal expenses. Any personal expenses run through the business (vehicles, travel, insurance, etc.) that would not be incurred by a new owner.
3.Related-party transactions. Above- or below-market transactions with related parties (rent, management fees, loans).
4.Non-recurring items. One-time expenses or income (lawsuit settlements, asset sales, insurance proceeds, PPP forgiveness).
5.Accounting method differences. Any differences between tax basis and GAAP/accrual basis accounting.
Why this matters: The QofE-normalized EBITDA is the primary input to the valuation. If the normalization is incomplete or inaccurate, the FMV may be understated or overstated — directly affecting the seller's proceeds and the ESOP's fiduciary compliance.
§ 04Timeline
{{materialsDueDate}}
{{appraiserName}} — {{appraiserEmail}}
{{qofeTargetDate}}
{{gate2Date}} — Valuation confirmed

Please deliver materials directly to the appraiser at the email above, and copy deals@forhemit.com for tracking. If you need additional time, please let us know as soon as possible — delays in the QofE directly impact the transaction timeline.

§ 05CPA Role in the Transaction

To clarify the CPA's role in this transaction:

A.You are providing data, not opinions. The CPA's role is to provide accurate financial data and working papers. The independent appraiser performs the QofE analysis and the valuation. The CPA does not determine FMV.
B.You may be asked to explain. The appraiser may have questions about specific line items, accounting methods, or adjustments. Please be available for a brief call if needed.
C.You will receive the final report. The appraiser's QofE report and valuation will be shared with you for review. You will have the opportunity to flag any factual errors before the report is finalized.
D.Your client is the seller. Forhemit coordinates the transaction, but your professional relationship is with the seller. Please advise the seller on any tax or accounting implications you identify.
§ 06Contact

For questions about this request or the transaction, please contact:

deals@forhemit.com
